data.title: FDI in China Slips 13.2% Despite Surge in High-Tech Sectors
data.description:
Foreign direct investment (FDI) in China declined by 13.2% year-on-year to CNY 358.19 billion (USD 49.88 billion) in the January–May 2025 period.

By sector, the manufacturing industry attracted CNY 91.52 billion in foreign capital, while the services sector received CNY 259.64 billion.

High-tech industries accounted for CNY 109.04 billion in actual foreign investment, with notable growth in specific areas: e-commerce services (146%), aerospace and equipment manufacturing (74.9%), chemical pharmaceutical manufacturing (59.2%), and medical equipment and device manufacturing (20%).

From a regional perspective, FDI from ASEAN countries rose by 20.5%, while inflows from Japan, the United Kingdom, South Korea, and Germany increased by 70.2%, 60.9%, 10.3%, and 7.1%, respectively.

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
Societe Generale in its early Friday economic news summary pointed out:

— Oil prices, US dollar retreat after President Trump gives Iran two weeks to negotiate end to the conflict with Israel. Discussions covering nuclear programme on Friday in Geneva between Iran, the United Kingdom, France, Germany, European Union officials and possibly China as a third party.

— Japan May headline consumer price index dips to 3.5% year over year from 3.6% in April. Core (excluding food) accelerates to 3.7% from 3.5%, the highest since January 2023, services tick up to 1.4%. Babj of Japan minutes: appropriate to raise rates if the economic outlook is met.

— U.K. retail sales plummet 2.7% month over month in May, the decline is first in five months. Sales excluding auto fuel slump 2.8%. Public deficit for FY25/26 widens to 37.7 billion pounds in May, below 40.7 billion pounds forecast by OBR.

— Day ahead: U.S. leading indicator, Philly Fed business outlook. European Central Bank economic outlook. Eurozone consumer confidence.

— Nikkei -0.1%, EUR 10-year IRS -2bps at 2.525%, Brent crude -2.7% at $76.7/barrel, Gold -0.8% at $3344/oz.

data.title: Gadang Unit Secures Over MYR92 Million Earthworks Deal
data.description:
Gadang unit Gadang Engineering (M) bagged a deal worth 92.5 million ringgit from AFA Construction and Engineering for earthworks and related jobs of the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Highway widening project.

The deal has a term of 18 months, and is expected to be completed by the last quarter of 2026, according to a Friday Malaysian bourse filing.

The contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's earnings.

data.title: Market Chatter: Germany's Boehringer Ingelheim to Invest $346 Million in Japan Plant for Asia-Oceania Expansion
data.description:
German pharmaceutical giant Boehringer Ingelheim will invest 300 million euros or $346 million in its Yamagata, Japan, plant from 2023 to 2028, positioning it as a key production hub for Asia and Oceania, Nikkei Asia reported Friday.

The facility currently manufactures the diabetes and kidney disease drug Jardiance, primarily for the Japanese market, said the publication, citing chairman Shashank Deshpande.

The investment includes 10 billion yen or $70 million previously announced in 2023 for expansion, with plans to construct a new building starting in 2026, while operations are expected to start by 2028, added the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: RBC Foresees 'Tough Print' for LVMH in H1; Price Target Down
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets said Friday it expects "a tough print" for LVMH in the first half amid headwinds faced by its fashion and leather goods segment.

For the second quarter, analysts expect the luxury goods giant to record a 7% annual decline in group revenue on a reported basis. Full-year estimates for revenue and adjusted EPS were also lowered.

"LVMH shares have relatively underperformed (-24% L3M) a fairly challenged luxury sector (-13%)," analysts said. "F&LG margins are approaching FY19 levels, and corroborating the 'over-earning' COVID-19 theory with the benefit of hindsight. Stock setup is modestly improving although not yet optimal, given light investor positioning, valuation towards 10yr lows (19.6x CY26E P/E) although earnings revisions are likely to remain negative."

On the back of the earnings downgrades, RBC lowered its price target on the French stock to 550 euros from 680 euros. The outperform rating was confirmed.

LVMH's first-half results will be published in July.

data.title: Emeren Group Agrees to be Acquired by Shurya Vitra in a Going Private Transaction
data.description:
Emeren Group said Thursday it has agreed to be acquired by Shurya Vitra for $0.20 per ordinary share, or $2 per American depositary share in cash.

If completed, the merger will result in the company becoming privately held, with its shares no longer listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

The company's board has approved the merger and also recommends its shareholders vote to approve the merger, which is expected to close during Q3.

Moreover, Himanshu Shah has entered an equity commitment letter to invest in Shurya Vitra to fund a portion of the merger to the extent necessary.

Emeren's shares were up over 6% in recent Friday premarket trading.

data.title: US Equity Futures Drop Pre-Bell After Trump Reportedly Sets 2-Week Timeframe for Iran Move
CZ Warns of AI Deepfake Threat After Top Crypto Influencer Hacked
data.description:
In a warning that’s catching attention across the crypto space, former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao (CZ) has raised the alarm about a new wave of AI-powered deepfake scams. Posting on X, CZ cautioned that even video call verifications may soon be unreliable.

He urged the community to avoid installing any software from unofficial links, especially when prompted during suspicious interactions.

Reason to panic? Here's everything we know.

Crypto Influencer Hacked After Deepfake Zoom Call

CZ’s warning came after Japanese crypto influencer Mai Fujimoto revealed that her main X account was hacked. The breach happened after she joined a Zoom call, believing she was speaking with a trusted friend.

What she didn’t know was that her contact’s Telegram account had already been compromised. During the call, a deepfaked version of the acquaintance told her to click a link to fix an audio issue. That’s when the attack happened.

'She shared a link for audio settings adjustment, and that's when the attack occurred,' Fujimoto wrote.

Soon after, her Telegram and Metamask accounts were also accessed, putting her crypto assets at risk.

A Growing Trend of AI-Powered Scams

Fujimoto’s trial is part of a disturbing pattern. On Thursday, reports surfaced of BlueNoroff, a North Korean-linked cybercrime group, targeting a cryptocurrency foundation employee with the same tactic. 

Over weeks of Zoom calls, the victim saw AI-generated deepfakes of company executives. A prompted download for a “microphone fix” installed a malicious extension, unleashing a keylogger, screen recorder, and crypto-stealing malware. 

These attacks show how AI can assist hackers in making scams, especially against crypto workers and remote employees.

Zhao Urges the Community to Stay Alert

CZ’s message is simple but serious: don’t trust software links from unofficial sources, and question the authenticity of video calls. As deepfake technology improves, online scams are becoming harder to detect.

For anyone in the crypto space, staying cautious is no longer optional. With AI making it easier for hackers to impersonate trusted contacts, protecting your assets now starts with verifying every link, call, and conversation.

data.title: Archer Aviation Partners with Dubai's Jetex to Expand Air Taxi Network
data.description:
Archer Aviation said Friday it entered into a partnership with Dubai, United Arab Emirates-based aviation services provider Jetex for infrastructure supporting the development of a commercial air taxi network.

The partners will design the passenger experience, deploy charging infrastructure and integrate the electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft company's Midnight vehicle into daily operations across Jetex's portfolio of 40 private aircraft terminals in over 30 countries, with an initial focus in the UAE.

data.title: FDI Into China Falls 13.2% in Jan-May
data.description:
Foreign Direct Investment in China dropped by 13.2% year-on-year to CNY 358.2 billion in the first five months of 2025, following a 10.9% fall in the January-April period.

The number of newly established foreign-invested enterprises nationwide climbed by 10.4% to 24,018.

By sector, direct investment in manufacturing reached CNY 91.52 billion, while investment in the service industry totaled CNY 259.64 billion.

High-tech industries attracted CNY 109.04 billion in foreign capital.

data.title: MAIV Announces Strategic Investment and Advisory Partnership with Covey
data.description:
BERLIN, June 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — MAIV, the on-chain platform for private equity deals, is proud to announce a strategic investment and advisory partnership with Covey, a web3-native firm specializing in advisory, capital, development, and incubation. Covey joins MAIV as both a capital partner and hands-on advisor, with a focus on scaling MAIV’s presence across institutional markets, ecosystem development, and token design.

The partnership reflects a strong alignment around MAIV’s vision to enable a new era of global finance through modular infrastructure for commercial deal tokenization and cross-border payments. With growing regulatory clarity and institutional appetite for compliant blockchain rails, MAIV is building the architecture to meet that demand head-on.

“With the upcoming launch of our testnet in July and our brand-new cross-border payment infrastructure rolling out later in the year, our partnership with Covey couldn’t come at a better time. Their extensive experience in scaling leading crypto projects across multiple fronts will be a key asset to MAIV, helping us strengthen our position and drive the success of our upcoming launches,” says Nick Taggart, MAIV founder and CEO.

“MAIV isn’t just building infrastructure—they’re laying the foundation for how value will move in the next era of global finance. From compliant RWA issuance to seamless cross-border settlement, the product vision is ambitious and the execution is world-class. Nick is one of the sharpest operators we’ve met in this space—clear, relentless, and deeply aligned with the scale of the opportunity. Backing MAIV was a no-brainer”, comments Hans Sundby, Co-Founder of Covey.

Specifically, Covey’s involvement will accelerate key aspects of MAIV’s roadmap, including narrative development, institutional onboarding, token design, and ecosystem partner integrations. With Covey’s deep experience across protocol design, investor strategy, and web3 storytelling, the collaboration is positioned to drive sustainable growth across both crypto-native and real-world financial markets.

This partnership reinforces MAIV’s long-term commitment to building infrastructure that is compliant by design, composable by architecture, and global by default.

About Covey

Covey is a strategic advisory and investment firm focused on early-stage crypto infrastructure and applications. The firm works hands-on with founders to craft positioning, token models, investor materials, and growth strategies—bridging the gap between vision and execution. Covey operates at the intersection of capital, communications, and product strategy to support the next generation of foundational web3 protocols.

About MAIV

MAIV democratizes access to private equity deals and enables investors of any size to participate in these highly lucrative deals backed with appraised hard assets. Tied to unique on-chain security tokens that provide legal rights to a specific allocation, commercial deals by MAIV combine institutional-grade yields and securities with the transparency, speed, and cost-efficiency of blockchain technology. Launching in Q2 2025, the MAIV platform will enable participation in new tokenized investment pools as well as the purchase and sale of stakes in existing contracts on its secondary market.

Media contacts :

MAIV

Simon Moser

Head of Communications

press@maiv.io

data.title: Ethereum Price Prediction 2025 Excites Investors, But Bitcoin Solaris Offers Immediate 150% Potential
data.description:
Ethereum is expected to rise by just over 3% in the coming months, according to recent forecasts. While that’s reassuring for long-term ETH holders, it pales in comparison to what’s unfolding around Bitcoin Solaris (BTC-S). With 150% potential locked into its launch structure and more than $5 million already raised, BTC-S isn’t a project investors are waiting to rise; it’s one they’re racing to join.

For those who missed out on ETH early or sold BTC too soon, this might be the cleanest second chance crypto has offered in years.

Ethereum Remains Solid, But Bitcoin Solaris Moves Faster

Ethereum is the second-largest cryptocurrency for a reason. It was the first to truly unlock decentralized smart contracts and continues to evolve with upgrades like staking and sharding. But even with all its influence, Ethereum still struggles with high gas fees, limited mobile accessibility, and occasional congestion.

That’s where Bitcoin Solaris is gaining attention. It solves problems Ethereum still faces while building for the mobile-first world of 2025 and beyond. It’s not just Ethereum’s future rewritten. It’s a smarter, fairer, more inclusive ecosystem.

Let’s compare the two:

FeatureEthereumBitcoin Solaris
TPS~15 (Layer 1)10,000+ with 2-second finality
ConsensusProof-of-StakeHybrid Proof-of-Work + DPoS
Energy UseModerateUltra-efficient hybrid model
Smart Contract LanguageSolidityRust
MiningNot availableAvailable via mobile + browser
Launch GainsOrganic growthStructured 150% presale ROI
Validator RotationStatic24-hour rotation for decentralization

Core Highlights of Bitcoin Solaris

http://bitcoinsolaris.comWhat makes BTC-S a standout in the 2025 altcoin space isn’t just price projections; it’s the depth of the ecosystem. From high-speed architecture to real-world accessibility, here are the most exciting features:

  • Dual-consensus model combining Bitcoin’s SHA-256 PoW with lightning-fast DPoS.
  • Validator rotation every 24 hours to maintain decentralization and reduce collusion.
  • Rust-based smart contracts, ideal for DeFi, NFTs, and enterprise-grade apps.
  • Bridge infrastructure for seamless cross-chain asset transfers.
  • Security features include zero-knowledge proof support, slashing for bad validators, and adaptive performance scoring for miners.

This isn’t theory. These features are already functional or actively under development, making BTC-S one of the most advanced blockchain launches of the year.

What Ethereum Promised, Bitcoin Solaris Is Actually Delivering

Wealth Building Through the Solaris Nova App

One of Bitcoin Solaris’s most powerful differentiators is its mining accessibility. Through the exciting release of the upcoming Solaris Nova app, users can mine BTC-S across smartphones, laptops, desktops, and even in-browser.

Benefits of this model include:

  • Real-time mining dashboards and adaptive energy-saving modes
  • Competitive leaderboards and gamification elements for consistent engagement
  • Smart contract-powered Mining Power Marketplace to buy or rent hash power
  • Easy earning estimation via the BTC-S mining calculator

The result is a mining system that isn’t exclusive to whales or tech experts; it’s for everyone.http://bitcoinsolaris.com

150% Presale Potential And Time’s Running Out

http://bitcoinsolaris.comThe BTC-S presale is currently in Phase 8, priced at $8. The next price jump to $9 is imminent, and the final launch price is locked at $20. That means:

  • Over $5M raised so far
  • 11,500+ unique users already in
  • Only 6 weeks left until the July 31, 2025 launch

What’s more, the presale is being called the shortest and most explosive in the space right now. It’s being featured across analyst channels like Crypto Show, where influencers are breaking down exactly why BTC-S has the mechanics to outperform.

Explore all the details via the official site: Bitcoin Solaris

Referral Rewards That Benefit Everyone

To accelerate ecosystem growth, Bitcoin Solaris has launched a unique dual-benefit referral program. This isn’t just another promo, it’s a full-scale incentive engine.

  • Referrers earn a 5% commission in BTC-S
  • Invitees receive an extra 5% bonus on their purchase
  • No lockups, fully liquid tokens from day one

This approach encourages long-term community building and makes it profitable to spread the word.

In addition, Holders get to win on a daily basis with the mini games bitcoin solaris introduced, checkout how to win here!

Final Verdict: Ethereum Is Growing Slowly, BTC-S Is Exploding Now

http://bitcoinsolaris.comEthereum’s predicted 3% gain is welcome, but it’s gradual. Meanwhile, Bitcoin Solaris is offering a structured, verified path to 150% returns within weeks, not months. It has a clear roadmap, real-time development momentum, and a mobile-first model that invites anyone to participate.

It’s not just the next big token. It’s a new way of building crypto wealth.

data.title: Miniso Group: Chairman's Decision to Unwind Collar Transaction and Regain Shares Shows Continued Confidence in Company >MNSO
Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0940 GMT - The Swiss franc is likely to strengthen if global conditions worsen as this could prompt global central banks to become more active in cutting interest rates, MUFG Bank's Derek Halpenny says in a note. The Swiss National Bank would potentially struggle to match global monetary policy easing, he says. The SNB cut rates by 25 basis points to 0% Thursday. If it shows a strong reluctance to negative rates, the only other alternative for curbing the franc's strength is foreign exchange interventions, he says. "Still, with trade negotiations between the U.S. and Switzerland ongoing, the scope for FX intervention may be limited especially after the U.S. put Switzerland on its watch list for currency manipulation." The euro rises 0.2% to 0.9406 francs. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0922 GMT - Berkeley has a reputation for shrewdly calling the housing market so its conservative guidance has made investors sit up and take notice, AJ Bell analysts Dan Coatsworth says in a note. The housebuilder guides for a substantial drop in pretax profit for fiscal 2026 and for profit to remain flat the year after, with the relatively downbeat outlook reflecting a volatile operating environment, Coatsworth says. "But the company is not letting short-term disappointment distract it from long-term goals. It is planning major investment and a big expansion in the build-to-rent market," Coatsworth says. Shares are down 6.8% at 3,866 pence, and are down 19% over the past 12 months. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0921 GMT - The cost of insuring euro credit against default using credit default swaps falls as concerns over the Israel-Iran conflict ease. "Investors found some relief after U.S. President Donald Trump signaled he would delay a decision on launching strikes against Iran for up to two weeks," IG analysts say in a note. The iTraxx Europe Crossover index, which tracks euro high-yield credit default swaps, declines 3 basis points to 308 bps, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0919 GMT - Berkeley's business model continues to deliver, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. Results for FY 2025 are in line with expectations and the housebuilder expects good underlying demand with transaction volumes gradually improving in FY 2026, the analysts say. Few housebuilders are providing guidance for FY 2026, and the company provides that, and next year's too. Pretax profit for FY 2026 is guided to be 450 million pounds, compared with Visible Alpha consensus at 468 million pounds, he says. As for the next year, it expects a similar result, with Visible Alpha consensus at 471 million pounds. "Berkeley's long forward orderbook is unique in the sector reflecting its differentiated business model, and it is a model which excels in challenging and uncertain markets," the analysts say. Shares are down 7.1% at 3,854 pence.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0838 GMT - Shares of semiconductor companies in Europe edge higher on Friday, following gains in the Stoxx Europe 600 and other European indexes. President Trump said he would decide on whether to strike Iran within two weeks. Meanwhile, European foreign ministers are slated to meet with Iranian officials in Geneva on Friday to press them to de-escalate. Shares of Dutch semiconductor-equipment maker ASML Holding and smaller rival ASM International are up 1.6%. German chip maker Infineon Technologies is up 1.9%. STMicroelectronics--which supplies Apple, Samsung Electronics and Tesla--is up 2%. The gains in Europe follow rises in Asian stocks like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., which closed 1.9% higher. South Korean memory-chip maker SK Hynix--which supplies Nvidia--closed 4.5% higher. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0836 GMT - There is high value in more predictable growth, which is what U.K. banks can provide in the next three years, Deutsche Bank says in a research note. U.K. banks' forecasted revenue growth in the midterm is the highest in Europe, analysts Robert Noble and Alfredo Alonso write. Despite this, U.K. banks trade at a discount to peers, the analysts say. "The outlook for the next three years is very favorable and we do see acquisitions as both likely and potentially accretive," they say. They prefer the superior and more predictable capital generation--their preferred metric to consider value creation--of NatWest and Lloyds, and Paragon among the specialty lenders. Acquisitions in the space are likely and potentially accretive. Barclays has the most incentive to do a deal, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0829 GMT - Investors may cut JD.com earnings' forecast in the near term due to food delivery competition, according to Nomura analysts Jialong Shi and Rachel Guo in a research note. The analysts point out gross merchandise value sales from on-demand delivery increased 19% on-year in the Jun. 18 promotion period, citing third party data. "We believe it is clear that the intensity of the competition raised by JD has way exceeded the market's initial expectation," the analysts say. Likewise, they are concerned that JD.com's two competitors, Alibaba Group and Meituan, may also have spent more than expected on their respective on-demand delivery business, Nomura analysts say. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0825 GMT - Berkeley continues to execute on its 10-year growth strategy, and this calls for investor patience, Quilter Cheviot analyst Oli Creasey says in a note. The housebuilder's key strategic pillar--the shift into build-to-rent--is an initiative that is still a few years from generating significant rental income, Creasey says. "While investors may note peers like Persimmon delivering profit growth... Berkeley is on a different trajectory--and direct comparisons may not be constructive," he says. Shares are down 7.1% at 3,856 pence and are down 19% over the past 12 months. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0825 GMT - Asian equity markets will likely see further volatility and they are highly dependent on tariff developments in 2H, Morningstar analyst Kai Wang says in a research note. The analyst notes that it took about a year for the market to bottom out during the first trade war. Morningstar is overweight on undervalued, high-quality and dividend-yielding stocks in defensive sectors given expectations of further uncertainty. When tariff risks ease, the brokerage would prefer tech and communication services. China's fiscal policy should shape the market if the country's macro economy remains challenged, the analyst says. "If fiscal policy is muted and underwhelming, then markets will likely see a pullback," he says. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0815 GMT - Doosan Fuel Cell is set to get a revenue boost from an expected increase in U.S.-bound shipments, NH Investment & Securities analysts write in a note. The South Korean fuel-cell maker will likely manufacture fuel cells for its U.S. subsidiary, HyAxiom, starting in 2H, they say. Analysts Y.S. Jung and J.H. Gwak raise their revenue forecast for the company by 8% for 2025 and 11% for 2026. They expect Doosan to win more contracts in the U.S., given the solid demand for power-generation facilities in data centers there. The U.S. fuel-cell market grows 15% to 20% annually, they note. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0809 GMT - European natural-gas prices fall more than 3% following Thursday's rally, as concerns over a potentially imminent U.S. intervention in Iran ease. Fears that escalating tensions between Tehran and Israel could disrupt LNG supplies during a key stockpiling period in Europe drove TTF prices up 8% in the previous trading session. "We believe that most of the price rally is risk premium driven, but lower Norwegian flows due to seasonal maintenance is also forcing additional spot LNG purchases which adds to the price pressure," analysts at DNB say. However, they note LNG imports remain significantly higher year-on-year and are sufficient to balance the market and refill inventories. The benchmark Dutch TTF contract is down 3.3% to 40.25 euros a megawatt-hour, though still on track for a weekly gain of 6%.(giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0801 GMT - Although Berkeley's strategy is ambitious, and there is a lot in its favor, it operates in a cyclical sector currently constrained by the wider economic environment, Interactive Investor Richard Hunter says. The housebuilder's shares have wilted given wider pressures despite its best efforts. They are currently down 33% from February 2020's prepandemic peak, and down 15% over the past year when compared to the 6.3% gain for the wider FTSE 100, he says. A low opening price is evidence that, for now, investors remain unconvinced by the outlook with its strategy still a distant dream, Hunter says. "As such, the market consensus of the shares is unlikely to break out of its current rating as a hold, albeit a strong one," he says. Shares are down 8.8% at 3,786 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)
